prompt_text:
Browser-evidence interpretation (fixture-only; no live browser). The deterministic trace JSON is committed at fixtures/browser_evidence/v1/export_flow.json. It includes navigation, console lines, optional dom_excerpts (visible text / HTML snippets), screenshot metadata (viewport, mime, integrity hash), and an extensions object for structured fields.

Summarize the navigation sequence; call out how dom_excerpts and screenshot metadata support grounding; list what you would still capture in a live Playwright trace; and note one limitation of fixture-only evidence versus a real browser session.

## Browser evidence (runner=file)

### Browser evidence (structured)
- **id:** evidence.export_flow.v1
- **title:** Abstract trace: Home → Settings → Export
- **navigation:**
  - https://example.test/ “Home” [navigate]
  - https://example.test/settings “Settings” [navigate]
  - https://example.test/settings/export “Export” [click]
- **console:**
  - [log] export clicked once
  - [log] no errors in console
- **dom_excerpts:**
  - **Export primary CTA** `button[data-testid='export']`
    - visible: Export
    - html: <button type="button" data-testid="export">Export</button>
  - **Settings heading** `main h1`
    - visible: Settings
- **screenshots:**
  - sha256 `e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855`; 1280×720; image/png; Fixture: metadata only (SHA-256 of empty buffer as placeholder).
- **extensions (JSON):**
{"fixture_profile": "export_flow.v1", "structured_capture_version": 1}
- **notes:** Deterministic fixture (no PII). Matches benchmark case.browser.evidence narrative.
